target: build-scripts/generate-html.js Overview evaluate-preface — applies the three mechanical fixes the curmudgeon required before integration: (1) re-anchor segment EP2 from the obsolete `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` rule (count=0 in current main) to the post-migration `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}` rule (count=1, verified at PRELUDE); (2) ds- prefix all 9 new CSS classes in BOTH the HTML class= attributes (segment EP1) and the CSS selectors (segment EP2) — closes the same design-system invariant ISS-1573 enforced against EXP-255; (3) switch color:var(--ink-3) -> color:var(--ink-2) on .ds-ep-heading / .ds-ep-num / .ds-ep-where to clear the dark-mode WCAG AA contrast gap (--ink-3 #8a8680 on --card-bg #222240 = 4.23:1 < 4.5:1 AA threshold; --ink-2 #b8b5ad on same = 7.48:1, well above AA). Plus one minor typography fix: ASCII apostrophe in question 2 ("model's") matching eg-evaluate body copy. SUBSTANCE UNCHANGED from EXP-257 — verbatim 5 questions, same link targets, same placement, same hub-and-spoke rationale.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: Confirmed unique at clone HEAD: `grep -c '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' build-scripts/generate-html.js` returns 1 (line 1463 in current main). Anchor unchanged from EXP-257 — the prefix migration did not touch this string. Pre-apply re-grep mandatory.
patch_segments.insert_text:
Five questions to ask of any scientific claim

From this review or any other — each question links to where this review tests it. Full preface →

  1. 1 Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? Standard Model Explains (3.4)
  2. 2 Can the prediction be derived from the model's own parameters? Self-Contradicted (3.3)
  3. 3 Is the cited data accurately represented? Refuted by Data (3.2)
  4. 4 Is the claim falsifiable? Misleading & Unfalsifiable (3.6)
  5. 5 Are the same data being counted multiple times? De-Duplication Analysis (6.6b)
patch_segments.collision_note: All 9 ds-prefixed class names verified to have 0 collisions in current build-scripts/generate-html.js: ds-evaluate-preface, ds-ep-heading, ds-ep-caption, ds-ep-jump, ds-ep-rows, ds-ep-row, ds-ep-num, ds-ep-q, ds-ep-where (each grep -c == 0 pre-apply). Distinct from .ds-vb-caption (verdict-bar caption), .ds-bn-header (breaking news), .ds-stance-statement (existing thesis-hero stance) etc. No CSS conflict expected.
changes_from_exp_257: Q2 typography: model’s -> model's (ASCII apostrophe matching eg-evaluate body copy at docs/index.html line 1574)

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: Confirmed unique at clone HEAD: `grep -c '\.ds-verdict-bars \.ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}' build-scripts/generate-html.js` returns 1 (line 553 in current main). Original EXP-257 anchor `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` returns 0 (broken by ISS-1571/1572/1573 ds- prefix migration). Pre-apply re-grep mandatory.
patch_segments.design_token_audit: All color values reference design tokens only (var(--ink-2), var(--ink-3), var(--accent), var(--heading), var(--border), var(--card-bg), var(--rule)). No hex literals. var(--ink-3) survives only as the LEFT-RAIL border color (border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3) on .ds-evaluate-preface) — borders are not subject to WCAG text-contrast rules; the rail is decorative and matches the eg-evaluate-preface visual quietness next to the EXP-256 falsifiability-module's accent rail.
changes_from_exp_257: EP2 anchor: .verdict-bars .vb-caption{...} -> .ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...} (the actual rule literal in current main)
.ds-ep-heading color: var(--ink-3) -> var(--ink-2)  (WCAG AA dark-mode fix)
.ds-ep-num color: var(--ink-3) -> var(--ink-2)  (WCAG AA dark-mode fix)
.ds-ep-where color: var(--ink-3) -> var(--ink-2)  (WCAG AA dark-mode fix)
.ds-evaluate-preface left-rail (border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3)) UNCHANGED — borders not subject to text-contrast rules

critical_1_css_anchor.curmudgeon_finding: Segment EP2 CSS anchor `.verdict-bars .vb-caption{...}` returns 0 grep hits in current generate-html.js. The ds- prefix migration (ISS-1571/1572/1573) renamed this to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}` after EXP-257 was drafted. A literal-anchor apply will silently no-op.
critical_1_css_anchor.fix: Re-anchored EP2 to `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}`. Verified `grep -c` == 1 at clone HEAD (line 553).
critical_1_css_anchor.verification: Pre-apply grep guard recommended for decider: refuse to apply EP2 unless grep -c == 1 for the new anchor literal.

moderate_1_ds_prefix.curmudgeon_finding: All 9 new CSS classes lack ds- design-system prefix. ISS-1573 (closed, moderate) explicitly enforced this invariant against EXP-255.
moderate_1_ds_prefix.fix: Renamed all 9 classes: .evaluate-preface, .ep-heading, .ep-caption, .ep-jump, .ep-rows, .ep-row, .ep-num, .ep-q, .ep-where -> .ds-evaluate-preface, .ds-ep-heading, .ds-ep-caption, .ds-ep-jump, .ds-ep-rows, .ds-ep-row, .ds-ep-num, .ds-ep-q, .ds-ep-where. Both segment EP1 (HTML class= attributes) and segment EP2 (CSS selectors) updated symmetrically.
moderate_1_ds_prefix.verification: Each ds-prefixed name verified to have 0 collisions in current generate-html.js (grep -c == 0 pre-apply).

moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.curmudgeon_finding: .ep-heading (0.82rem 600), .ep-num (0.78rem 700), .ep-where (0.72rem 600 uppercase) all use color:var(--ink-3). Dark mode --ink-3 (#8a8680) on --card-bg (#222240) computes to 4.23:1 — below the AA 4.5:1 threshold for normal text. None qualify as WCAG large text (would need >= 14pt bold ~ 18.7px or 18pt regular). Reproduces ISS-1580 (open, moderate) for EXP-256.
moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.fix: Switched the three .ds-ep-* color declarations from var(--ink-3) to var(--ink-2). Dark mode --ink-2 (#b8b5ad) on --card-bg (#222240) computes to 7.48:1 (well above AA). Light mode --ink-2 (#3A352D) on --card-bg (#fafafa) computes to ~10.4:1 (was 5.36:1 with --ink-3 — still passes AA, slightly stronger contrast). .ds-ep-heading, .ds-ep-num, .ds-ep-where remain typographically distinct from --text and --heading via size/weight/transform.
moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.verification: Curmudgeon-cited fix option 1 (option 2 — global --ink-3 dark-mode bump — would require coordination with ISS-1580 and was deferred). The decorative left-rail border (border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3)) is unaffected by WCAG text-contrast rules.
moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.side_effect_iss_1580: ISS-1580 (EXP-256 falsifiability-module fm-status-num/fm-status-sub) is NOT closed by this rework — the falsifiability-module's --ink-3 text remains. ISS-1580 should be tracked as a separate follow-up; if a global --ink-3 dark-mode bump is preferred, both can be resolved in one pass.

minor_1_apostrophe.curmudgeon_finding: Question 2 typography divergence: patch uses model’s (curly entity), eg-evaluate uses model's (ASCII straight). Disclosed in EXP-257 load_bearing_content_audit but creates micro-inconsistency between Overview-preface and Evaluation-Guide-tab body copy.
minor_1_apostrophe.fix: ASCII apostrophe applied: `model's` (matching eg-evaluate body copy at docs/index.html line 1574). Lowest-effort option per curmudgeon recommendation.
minor_1_apostrophe.verification: Sub-2-byte change. No layout or contrast impact.

minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED.curmudgeon_finding: Caption link `Full preface ->` lands on a closed `<details id="eg-evaluate">` element. Reader sees the section anchor but the long-form is collapsed by default. Implies one click; in fact two (jump + expand).
minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED.decision: DEFERRED — not applied in this rework.
minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED.rationale: The two curmudgeon options are (a) caption verbiage rewrite ('Read the long form ->') or (b) augment showTab('evaluate') with details.open expansion for #eg-evaluate when arriving via the preface jump. Option (a) is a copy judgment call where 'Full preface ->' is also defensible (it is, in fact, what the link reaches — the full preface section, not its body). Option (b) is more invasive and risks unexpected expand-on-click behavior elsewhere. Both are explicitly minor in the curmudgeon's review and the curmudgeon's `summary_for_decider` listed only the three mechanical fixes (critical anchor, moderate ds-prefix, moderate WCAG) as required. Per rework-EXP convention (fix blockers, defer judgment-call optionals), deferring this here keeps the rework scope tight. Operator/decider may add a follow-up issue if they prefer either option.
minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED.escape_hatch_for_defender: A defender could argue 'while we are reworking, fix it.' That is fair, and the change is trivial (3-byte string swap). The reason this rework declines is that it is a copy-tone choice between three reasonable variants (Full preface, Read the long form, Continue to the long form), and the analyst should not silently pick one when none is clearly correct. Routing it as a future minor issue gives the operator agency.

load_bearing_content_audit.five_questions_verbatim_status: Identical to EXP-257 Q1, Q3, Q4, Q5 (curmudgeon confirmed exact verbatim match in five_question_verbatim_audit). Q2 changed only in apostrophe encoding (’ -> ASCII '), eliminating the typography divergence the curmudgeon flagged in minor #1. The text content of all five questions remains verbatim from eg-evaluate ... opening clauses (docs/index.html line 1574 area).
load_bearing_content_audit.link_targets_status: All 6 link targets unchanged from EXP-257 (curmudgeon-verified all 6 resolve at clone HEAD): #stdmodel, #selfcon, #refuted, #misleading, #dedup-analysis, #eg-evaluate. All 5 showTab() routes unchanged: 'wins' x4, 'predictions' x1. Caption showTab('evaluate') unchanged.
load_bearing_content_audit.placement_status: Unchanged. EP1 inserts BEFORE the unique anchor `<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section"...>` — same anchor as EXP-257. Stack order Overview tab post-integration: verdict-bar -> falsifiability-module (EXP-256) -> thesis-hero (EXP-255) -> evaluate-preface (this rework) -> accuracy-math-section.

cross_references.parent_proposal: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-257-overview-evaluate-preface.json (substance approved by curmudgeon c1; do NOT apply verbatim)
cross_references.closed_issue_precedent_ds_prefix: monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json ISS-1573 (severity moderate, status fixed) — same invariant against EXP-255
cross_references.open_issue_co_pending: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json ISS-1580 (severity moderate, status open) — same dark-mode WCAG gap against EXP-256; not closed by this rework, see fixes_applied.moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.side_effect_iss_1580
sibling_reworks: EXP-261 (parent EXP-255, overview-hero-rework-v2) — same rework pattern: substance approved, mechanical fixes applied
EXP-256 (falsifiability-module) — sibling in the EXP-255/256/257 trio; ISS-1580 still open against it

kernel_of_truth.description: The 5-question methodology in eg-evaluate (with the medicine analogy) is the most generalizable scientific-thinking content in the review. It works whether the reader cares about the dome model or any other claim. Today, it lives entirely in the Evaluation Guide tab — a casual Overview-only reader never sees the framework that the rest of the review applies. Promoting it to an Overview preface materially improves discoverability and gives the reader navigational shortcuts to the verdict buckets that demonstrate each failure mode.
kernel_of_truth.why_kernel_doesnt_break_module: Curmudgeon c1 (qid 268) confirmed substance is sound: questions verbatim, link targets resolve, placement story sound, hub-and-spoke redundancy with eg-evaluate is acceptable. The blockers were purely mechanical (anchor, prefix, contrast). All three are fixable in one decider pass without touching content, which is exactly what this rework does.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why didn't EXP-257 catch the ds- prefix migration at draft time?
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-257 was drafted at 2026-04-26T04:08Z. The ISS-1571/1572/1573 ds- prefix migration landed AFTER (the EXP-255/256/257 trio was queued at 2026-04-26T05:24Z). The proposal's anchor_uniqueness check was true at draft time but invalidated by the prefix migration before the curmudgeon review fired. Curmudgeon's stronger_arguments[0] explicitly recommended adding pre-write grep self-tests to proposal packages to catch this race condition automatically. That is a meta-fix beyond this rework's scope but flagged as a follow-up tinker concern.

anticipated_objections.objection: If the rework switches three colors from --ink-3 to --ink-2, the .ds-ep-heading / .ds-ep-num / .ds-ep-where will be visually less differentiated from body text in light mode (--ink-2 #3A352D vs body --text #222 — much closer than --ink-3 #6E675B was).
anticipated_objections.response: Quantitative check: light mode --text is #222 (RGB 34/34/34, luminance ~0.0189). --ink-2 #3A352D (RGB 58/53/45, luminance ~0.0414) sits 2.2x lighter than body text but still much darker than --heading #2E4057. Differentiation is preserved by font-weight (600/700 vs body 400) and size/transform (uppercase, smaller px) on .ds-ep-heading and .ds-ep-where; .ds-ep-num is right-aligned tabular-nums, also visually distinct. Side-by-side is fine. Curmudgeon's fix_options[0] explicitly noted --ink-2 'still distinct from --text and --heading' for this reason.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also fix the .ds-evaluate-preface left-rail color (border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3)) for visual consistency with the new --ink-2 text?
anticipated_objections.response: Borders are not subject to WCAG text-contrast rules. The left-rail is intentionally quiet (--ink-3 — a neutral) to differentiate the preface from the EXP-256 falsifiability-module's --accent left-rail. Changing the rail to --ink-2 would tighten visual quietness only marginally and could make the preface visually compete with adjacent modules. Curmudgeon flagged only text-color issues, not the rail. Decision: leave rail as --ink-3.

anticipated_objections.objection: Did the rework introduce any net-new content versus EXP-257 substance?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The five questions, link targets, placement, hub-and-spoke caption, all 5 deep links, and the @media(max-width:720px) responsive collapse are unchanged. Only mechanical/typographic transforms: 9 class renames, 1 CSS anchor swap, 3 color-token swaps, 1 apostrophe encoding fix. The HTML word_count_per_analyst (110) is identical to EXP-257; CSS byte count grew from 1500 to ~1535 due to longer ds-prefixed selectors. The patch remains additive-only.

anticipated_objections.objection: ISS-1580 (EXP-256 dark-mode WCAG) is still open. Why not roll a global --ink-3 dark-mode bump in this rework and close both at once?
anticipated_objections.response: Coordination risk. A global --ink-3 dark-mode bump would touch every existing use site of --ink-3 in dark mode (.accuracy-math-section .ams-heading, .accuracy-math-section .ams-variant, .accuracy-math-section .ams-cross, plus any other downstream uses). That is a sweep-fix that should be (a) reviewed in its own rework or issue-resolution, (b) tested for regressions across the whole Overview, and (c) potentially decided by the operator since it changes the look of multiple already-shipped modules. The curmudgeon's fix_options[0] (local --ink-2 swap) is the surgical option and what this rework applies. ISS-1580 remains open with explicit reference to the global option.

anticipated_objections.objection: The deferred caption-verbiage minor (`Full preface ->` vs `Read the long form ->`) is a 3-byte change. Why not just apply it?
anticipated_objections.response: Because it is a tone choice, not a defect. 'Full preface ->' is literally accurate (the link reaches the full preface section). 'Read the long form ->' is more honest about the two-click reality (jump + expand). 'Continue to the long form ->' is yet another reasonable option. The analyst declines to silently pick one when the curmudgeon explicitly listed it as 'Optional'. The other_side_of_the_argument here is captured in fixes_applied.minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED.escape_hatch_for_defender. Operator can add a follow-up issue with their preferred wording.

other_side_of_the_argument: Strongest defender pushback against this rework: 'You are doing too little — fix the deferred minors and roll the global --ink-3 dark-mode bump while you're in there.' Counter: minor #2 is a copy judgment-call where the analyst should not silently choose; ISS-1580 (global --ink-3 fix) needs operator coordination because it changes the look of already-shipped modules. The rework deliberately stays surgical so the decider can apply it as a single mechanical pass without re-opening EXP-257's substance review. A second defender pushback: 'Why not just have the curmudgeon's loop catch this on cycle 2 — why a separate rework EXP at all?' Counter: cycle 2 against unchanged substance would still flag the same critical/moderates and the EXP would idle in the queue for another full curmudgeon pass; the rework path (EXP-261/EXP-264 pattern) is faster and clearer about what changed since c1.

decider_instructions: REPLACES the EXP-257 verbatim apply path. Apply EXP-264.patch_segments to build-scripts/generate-html.js (HTML insert before the unique anchor `<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">`; CSS insert after the unique anchor `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}`). Both anchors verified unique (grep -c == 1) at clone HEAD; pre-apply re-grep mandatory. After applying, run `node build.js html` to regenerate docs/index.html, then `node test.js` to confirm no regressions. Curmudgeon-recommended sequence (still applies): if EXP-255 / EXP-256 are also pending, integrate one at a time and re-grep anchors between commits. Suggested curmudgeon routing post-integration: cycle-2 spot-check on the same EXP-257 queue_id (target_type=overview-evaluate-preface, focus on integration drift / WCAG contrast / class-collision after-the-fact). EXP-257 should be marked superseded-by EXP-264 in the tracker once EXP-264 is integrated.
files_to_commit: monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (EXP-264 status -> complete; EXP-257 left as proposal-superseded for traceability)
build_impact: Pure presentation-layer additive insert (HTML + CSS). No data dependencies, no template variables, no monitor.py / pull_data.py interactions. Identical build_impact to EXP-257 substance.
out_of_scope_explicit: Caption verbiage tone change (deferred minor — see fixes_applied.minor_2_caption_verbiage_DEFERRED).

Global --ink-3 dark-mode token bump (would close ISS-1580 in the same pass; deferred to a separate sweep-rework with operator coordination — see fixes_applied.moderate_2_dark_mode_wcag.side_effect_iss_1580).
Pre-write grep self-test additions to the proposal package format (curmudgeon stronger_arguments[0] meta-recommendation — flagged as tinker follow-up; not in this rework).
Auto-expand details.open for #eg-evaluate on showTab('evaluate') from the preface jump (curmudgeon's alternative for minor #2 — more invasive UX change, not in this rework).
Any change to the substance / link targets / placement / questions of EXP-257 — those were curmudgeon-approved.
Resolution of ISS-1580 (open against EXP-256). Tracked separately.
notes_for_decider: EXP-264 is a pure mechanical rework of EXP-257. The summary_for_decider in the curmudgeon review explicitly says 'DO NOT apply patch verbatim' and lists the three required fixes — all three are applied here, plus minor #1 (apostrophe). EXP-257 should remain in the tracker as historical substance-approved-but-superseded, not deleted. Pre-apply re-grep is the only failure mode left: if any other rework lands between this rework and EXP-264 integration that touches the EP2 anchor `.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{...}` rule, the apply will silently no-op the same way EXP-257 would have. Decider's pre-apply grep guard should refuse to proceed if grep -c != 1 for either anchor.
